items.title: 'Happy to take many more': Rahul Gandhi on being 'punched' during Lok Kalyan Marg protest
items.summary: Calling it an honour to stand with the people, the Congress leader said, couple of punches was not a problem at all. “It's okay, I am used to it,” Gandhi said during a pres conference on Wednesday.

items.title: Lok Sabha adopts motion to suspend TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee for Monsoon Session over remarks against woman member
items.summary: Lok Sabha on Wednesday passed a resolution suspending TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee for the remainder of the Monsoon Session over alleged use of unparliamentary language against a woman member.

items.summary: The protest was unprecedented in that no Leader of the Opposition has in recent times staged a protest on the high-security stretch – Lok Kalyan Marg, earlier known as Race Course Road – which houses the official residence of the prime minister.

items.summary: Several opposition leaders—including Arvind Kejriwal, Uddhav Thackeray and Mamata Banerjee—have extended their support to Wangchuk, with most urging him to end his fast.

items.title: Parliament Monsoon Session: BJP wooing NCP (SP), DMK for Constitution Amendment Bill, alleges Chidambaram
items.summary: The failed Bill purported to reserve for women one-third of the seats in the Lok Sabha and the state assemblies, but its real purpose was to pave the way for delimitation and, possibly, gerrymandering of constituencies, he said.

items.title: TN schools now ‘no-politics’ zone. Vijay b’day event, ex-AIADMK MLA’s PIL forced TVK govt’s hand
items.summary: School Education Department issued circular 10 July that stated ‘any sort of celebrations or messaging should not be permitted on school campuses'.

items.title: West Bengal withdraws plea in SC against HC verdict striking down OBC status of 77 castes
items.summary: West Bengal withdraws plea in SC against a high court verdict striking down OBC status of 77 castes. The pleas were filed against the high court verdict during the previous government led by Mamata Banerjee.

items.title: CPM leader arrested in Baruipur mob violence case: Who is Md Lahek Ali?
Why Rahul Gandhi is refusing to blink on Raja Warring despite Punjab Congress’s growing revolt
items.summary: Gandhi’s backing of Warring is no longer just about the future of one Pradesh Congress Committee president. It has become a test of the Congress leadership's authority itself.

items.title: ‘India’s Got Latent’ row: SC imposes Rs 3 lakh cost on Samay Raina for ‘taking court for a ride’
items.summary: The cost of Rs 3 lakh was also imposed on four other comedians for not complying with the Supreme Court’s reparation order.

items.title: Arvind Kejriwal writes to PM Modi on E20 concerns, demands pure petrol option at pumps
items.summary: The AAP chief said that the price of ethanol-blended fuel should be reduced to reflect the ‘lower calorific value and reduced mileage’ it delivers.

items.title: Why the ED is going after a BJP leader in Mahadev betting app case
items.summary: Vikas Garg was appointed the convenor of BJP Delhi’s economic cell in 2024. He now faces money laundering charges in a case related to an illegal betting app.

items.title: SC refuses to restore Friday namaz at Bhojshala, allows Muslim prayers near disputed site
items.summary: The bench was hearing a challenge to the Madhya Pradesh High Court’s May ruling that the complex in Dhar district is a temple of Hindu deity Saraswati.

items.title: Karnataka HC criticises ‘adjournment culture’, questions 12-year delay in Pocso trial
items.summary: Such delays are a ‘silent accomplice to miscarriage of justice’, the High Court said.

items.title: Withdraw mandatory declaration on parents’ inclusion in previous SIR, CPI(M) MP urges EC
items.summary: John Brittas said that the ‘arbitrary requirement’ would create an unjust burden on millions of people who may not have access to decades-old electoral records.

items.title: BJP councillor takes oath in jail, but party still faces uncertainty in Thiruvananthapuram corporation
items.summary: Sugathan was arrested in June in an attempt-to-murder case. In the event of his disqualification, BJP will be left without majority in Thiruvananthapuram corporation.

items.title: Supreme Court upholds Lalu Yadav’s bail in Deoghar fodder scam case
items.summary: The matter pertains to the alleged embezzlement of Rs 89 lakh from the Deoghar treasury between 1991 and 1994, when he was the chief minister of Bihar.

items.title: Against the flow: Union minister and Beant Singh grandson Ravneet Singh Bittu declares war on Satluj
items.summary: Rajya Sabha member goes whole hog with social media, press meets and interviews to create counter-narrative against what he calls ‘selective retelling’, and ‘half the story’.

items.summary: A Uniform Civil Code bill is expected to be tabled in the upcoming Monsoon Session.

items.summary: Wangchuk, 59, has lost 8.25 kg, his blood sugar levels have repeatedly fallen below 70 mg/dL (normal fasting levels are around 100 mg/dL), and he is experiencing constant dizziness, severe muscle loss and visible weakness, with his rib cage now clearly visible, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke said.

items.title: 2020 Delhi riots: Ex-AAP councillor Tahir Hussain among five convicted for IB staffer’s murder
items.summary: Ankit Sharma, who worked at the Intelligence Bureau, was killed by a mob during the violence in the national capital.

items.title: From BJP’s ‘bulldozer man’ in MP to the margins: Why Narottam Mishra broke down after losing Datia ticket
items.summary: Mishra had begun preparations to fight bypoll to Datia—a constituency he represented for multiple terms & thought he was BJP’s obvious choice for. But he was denied a ticket.

items.title: Supreme Court stays Madras HC order banning cow slaughter in Tamil Nadu
items.summary: The High Court had directed the state to ensure that no cow or calf is slaughtered on the eve of Bakrid or any other day.

items.title: Honoured to receive BJP ‘love letter’ as ₹100 cr legal notice: Omar Abdullah amid 'MLA poaching' row
items.summary: The J&K unit of the Bharatiya Janata Party on Monday served a legal notice to Abdullah over his claim that the national party had attempted to bribe National Conference legislators with cash and ministerial berths in a bid to topple his government.

items.summary: The addition has been introduced without any corresponding amendment to the statutory Form 6. Although the field is not marked as compulsory, users cannot proceed on ECINET portal with the application unless they respond.

items.summary: In his address, PM Modi described India and Australia as ‘natural and trusted partners’, saying the two countries should deepen cooperation as the world grapples with uncertainty, supply chain disruptions and an energy crisis.
